question:
Suppose x, y, and z are variables of type int. Assume x != y.
Which of the following expressions is true exactly when z is between x and y, not including x and y?

I. (x < z) && (z < y)
II. (y < z) && (z < x)
III. ((x < z) && (z < y)) || ((y < z) && (z < x))

choices: A. c and d are both 1.75
B. c is 1.0 and d is 1.75
C. c is 1.75 and d is 1.0
D. c is 1.0 and d is 1.0
answer: B. c is 1.0 and d is 1.75

choices: A. Returns true when num is even
B. Returns true when num is prime
C. Returns true when num is composite
D. Returns true when num is positive
answer: B. Returns true when num is prime
